page: Vultr vcg-a40-8c-40g-16vram is a Cloud GPU family server configured with fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU resources. It provides a 0.3333 slice of an A40 GPU, delivering 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor offering 8 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration.

The instance features 40.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 5.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 740 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, meaning the 8 vCPUs share physical host resources with other tenants, which may impact workloads requiring sustained, predictable CPU performance.

By utilizing a fractional GPU slice rather than a full dedicated card, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not saturate a full A40 GPU. This resource density makes it appropriate for small-scale machine learning inference, model prototyping, data analysis, and hardware-accelerated rendering tasks that fit within the 16 GB VRAM and 40.0 GB system memory limits.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-8c-40g-16vram is a Cloud GPU instance featuring a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. The server is equipped with an Intel x86_64 processor configured with 8 vCPUs across 8 physical cores, operating with a shared CPU allocation. It includes 40.0 GB of system memory, providing 5.0 GB per core, alongside 740 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address. The fractional GPU architecture offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the expense of a dedicated card. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a performance tradeoff for CPU-bound tasks. This configuration is suitable for small-scale machine learning inference, model prototyping, and hardware-accelerated rendering.

tagline: A fractional GPU instance combining a slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 with shared Intel vCPUs and NVMe storage.
